evidenceAudit.score_formula_status: Norm bands and demo scores are visible in the public client; raw-to-normalized, percentile, composite-domain, domain, and overall score formulas are not exposed.

mcmahon_cmj_force_time.note: The extracted app linked the CMJ force-time article to PMID 30479481, which is unrelated. The corrected source is the Strength and Conditioning Journal article page.

dossantos_imtp.note: PMID 28002178 supports IMTP onset-threshold methodology. It does not validate all score bands or category thresholds.

buchheit_laursen_hiit.note: PMID 23539308 is Part I and PMID 23832851 is Part II of the HIIT programming review. These support prescription concepts, not final HPR score thresholds.

haugen_sprint_mechanics.note: The extracted app linked an elite sprint-mechanics claim to PMID 31034252. PMID 31339890 is the verified Haugen elite-athlete sprint mechanical variables paper.

sedentary.rationale: Norms derived from age/sex-matched inactive adult populations. Thresholds represent minimum functional capacity targets. Scores emphasise health-related fitness, fall risk, and ADL performance.
sedentary.scoring_note: A score of 5/10 = meets minimum functional standard. Below 5 = screening concern pending direct threshold validation.

references.text: ACSM Guidelines for Exercise Testing & Prescription, 12th Ed.
Rikli RE & Jones CJ (2013). Senior Fitness Test Manual, 2nd Ed. Human Kinetics.
Blackwell JR et al. (2020). DYNAVISION D2 normative data, adults 18–80 yr. AJOT.

trained.rationale: Norms derived from recreational athlete populations. Thresholds represent healthy active adult standards. Scores reflect performance across sport science literature.
trained.scoring_note: A score of 7/10 = meets recreational athlete standard. Below 6 = potential training gap.

references.text: ACSM Guidelines for Exercise Testing & Prescription, 12th Ed.
Kenney WL, Wilmore JH, Costill DL (2020). Physiology of Sport and Exercise, 7th Ed. Human Kinetics.
McMahon JJ et al. (2018). Understanding the key phases of the CMJ force-time curve. Strength Cond J.

competitive.rationale: Norms derived from competitive athlete databases. Thresholds represent expected ranges for sport-specific performance. Source copy describes sport-demand weighting, but the public client does not expose the weighting formula.
competitive.scoring_note: A score of 7.5/10 = competitive standard. Below 7 = targeted intervention required.

references.text: NSCA — Essentials of Strength Training and Conditioning, 4th Ed.
Dos'Santos T et al. (2017). Effect of different onset thresholds on IMTP force-time variables. J Strength Cond Res.
Buchheit M & Laursen PB (2013). High-intensity interval training, solutions to the programming puzzle. Part I: cardiopulmonary emphasis. Sports Med.

elite.rationale: Norms derived from elite/professional athlete databases. Thresholds represent top-percentile performance. Marginal gains logic applied — small improvements are clinically meaningful.
elite.scoring_note: A score of 8.5/10 = elite standard. Any domain below 8 = priority intervention.

references.text: Buchheit M (2014). Monitoring training status with HR measures: do all roads lead to Rome? Front Physiol.
Haugen T et al. (2019). Sprint mechanical variables in elite athletes: are force-velocity profiles sport specific or individual? PLoS One.
Squeo MR et al. (2025). Cardiopulmonary Exercise Testing in Elite Athletes: Rethinking Sports Classification. J Clin Med.
ACSM Guidelines for Exercise Testing & Prescription, 12th Ed.

cmj_peak_force_relative.label: CMJ Peak Force (relative to BM)
cmj_peak_force_relative.notes: Peak GRF ÷ body weight (N). Typical range 1.5–2.5× BW. Linthorne (2001); McMahon et al. (2018); Gathercole et al. (2015).

cmj_jump_height.label: CMJ Jump Height
cmj_jump_height.notes: Impulse-momentum method. Mixed sex norms. Sayers et al. (1999); NSCA 2024. M norms ~5–8 cm higher than F.

cmj_eccentric_rfd.label: CMJ Eccentric (Braking) RFD
cmj_eccentric_rfd.notes: Peak braking RFD from CMJ force-time curve. Reflects SSC loading capacity. McMahon et al. (2018).

reactive_strength_index.label: Reactive Strength Index (RSI)
reactive_strength_index.notes: Drop jump 30 cm box. RSI = jump height (m) ÷ GCT (s). Flanagan & Comyns (2008); Beattie et al. (2017).

imtp_peak_force_relative.label: IMTP Peak Force (relative to BM)
imtp_peak_force_relative.notes: Net peak force ÷ body mass. Hip angle 145°. Dos'Santos et al. (2017); Haff et al. (2015); Comfort et al. (2019).

imtp_rfd_100ms.label: IMTP RFD (0–100 ms)
imtp_rfd_100ms.notes: Onset threshold 75 N above BW. Khamoui et al. (2011); Haff et al. (2015); NSCA 2024.

grip_strength.label: Grip Strength (dominant)
grip_strength.notes: Seated, elbow 90°. ACSM; Norman et al. (2011). <25 kg (F) / <30 kg (M) = dynapenia threshold.

symmetry_index.label: Force Symmetry Index (CMJ bilateral)
symmetry_index.notes: Stored as % symmetry (100 = perfect bilateral). SI% = 100 − (|L−R| / max(L,R)) × 100. CMJ preferred for functional assessment. Impellizzeri et al. (2007).

bilateral_deficit_index.label: Bilateral Deficit Index (SJ)
bilateral_deficit_index.notes: BDI = (bilateral SJ − 2× unilateral SJ) / (2× unilateral SJ) × 100. Negative = bilateral deficit (neuromuscular inhibition). Jakobi & Chilibeck (2001); Howard & Enoka (1991).

tug_test.label: Timed Up and Go (TUG)
tug_test.notes: Rise from chair, walk 3 m, turn, return and sit. ACSM gold-standard fall-risk screen. >12 s = high fall risk. Podsiadlo & Richardson (1991); ACSM 12th Ed.

single_leg_balance.label: Single-Leg Balance (eyes closed)
single_leg_balance.notes: Unipedal stance, eyes closed, arms folded. <10 s = significant proprioceptive deficit. Hurvitz et al. (2000); ACSM fall-risk protocol. Best of 3 trials per limb.

range_of_motion.label: Joint Range of Motion (hip/knee flexion composite)
range_of_motion.notes: Hip + knee composite ROM. ACSM Guidelines 12th Ed.; Clarkson & Gilewich (2000). Reduced ROM linked to movement dysfunction and fall risk.

movement_velocity.label: Movement / Gait Velocity
movement_velocity.notes: Self-selected gait speed (10 m walk test). <0.8 m/s = fall-risk threshold (ACSM). Studenski et al. (2011) JAMA — gait speed as vital sign.

temporal_symmetry.label: Temporal Gait Symmetry (step time ratio)
temporal_symmetry.notes: Ratio of L:R step times. <0.90 = clinically significant asymmetry (Hodt-Billington et al., 2008). Used in post-injury return-to-sport and fall-risk screening.

cadence.label: Cadence (steps per minute)
cadence.notes: Steps per minute during preferred gait. WHO physical activity norms; Tudor-Locke et al. (2019). ≥100 steps/min = moderate-intensity walking threshold.

muscle_activation_timing.label: EMG Muscle Onset Timing
muscle_activation_timing.notes: Quad/hamstring onset relative to perturbation. Neuromuscular coordination marker. Sport-literature marker; exact score threshold still under audit.

movement_smoothness.label: Movement Smoothness (SPARC)
movement_smoothness.notes: Spectral arc length metric. Morin & Samozino (2016). 0 = perfect smoothness; more negative = more jerk.

reaction_time.label: Reaction Time (DYNAVISION D2, T-Mode)
reaction_time.notes: T-Mode, 60 s, all 5 rings. Blackwell et al. (2020) AJOT — adults 18–40 yr median ~377 ms standing.

gait_speed.label: Self-Selected Gait Speed
gait_speed.notes: 10 m walk test. PubMed spatiotemporal norms (2024). <0.8 m/s = fall-risk threshold (ACSM).

stride_length.label: Stride Length
stride_length.notes: Mean of ≥6 strides. Context-dependent (walking vs running). Spatiotemporal norms (PubMed 2024).

ground_contact_time.label: Ground Contact Time (running)
ground_contact_time.notes: Treadmill running. Lower GCT = better elastic energy utilisation. Haugen et al. (2020). Note: walking GCT ~550–700 ms is normal; this metric applies to running.

emg_onset_timing.label: EMG Muscle Onset Timing
emg_onset_timing.notes: Quad/hamstring onset relative to perturbation. Neuromuscular coordination marker. Sport-literature marker; exact score threshold still under audit.

vo2max.label: VO2max
vo2max.notes: ACSM 12th Ed. norms (18–45 yr mixed sex). Squeo et al. (2025) elite reference. RER ≥1.10 for maximal confirmation.

vt1.label: Ventilatory Threshold 1 (VT1)
vt1.notes: V-slope / ventilatory equivalents method. Zone 2 training anchor. Typically 50–65% VO2max. Buchheit & Laursen (2013).

vt2.label: Ventilatory Threshold 2 (VT2 / MLSS)
vt2.notes: Second inflection in VE/VCO2. Maximal lactate steady state proxy. Typically 80–90% VO2max. Buchheit & Laursen (2013); Laursen (2019).

hr_recovery.label: HR Recovery (1 min post-exercise)
hr_recovery.notes: Peak HR − HR at 60 s passive recovery. Cardiac vagal reactivation marker. ACSM; Cole et al. (1999).

submaximal_hr.label: Submaximal HR at Fixed Work Rate
submaximal_hr.notes: HR at standardised work rate (M: 150 W / F: 100 W trained; M: 200 W / F: 150 W elite). 5 min steady-state. Longitudinal monitoring metric.

o2_pulse.label: O2 Pulse (VO2/HR — stroke volume proxy)
o2_pulse.notes: VO2 ÷ HR at peak effort. Non-invasive stroke volume proxy. Derived from CPET data. Arena et al. (2007). <10 mL/beat = poor cardiac output reserve.

ve_vco2_slope.label: VE/VCO2 Slope (Ventilatory Efficiency)
ve_vco2_slope.notes: Slope of VE vs VCO2 during incremental exercise. >34 = impaired ventilatory efficiency (possible cardiac/pulmonary limitation). Arena et al. (2007); AHA CPET guidelines.

processing_speed.label: Processing Speed
processing_speed.notes: Pattern Comparison Processing Speed Test. Age/education corrected. NIH Toolbox CB V3 Norming (2025).

executive_function.label: Executive Function (Flanker Inhibitory Control)
executive_function.notes: Flanker Inhibitory Control & Attention Test. Age-corrected composite. NIH Toolbox CB V3 (2025).

dual_task_cost.label: Dual-Task Cost (D2 + secondary task)
dual_task_cost.notes: DTC% = (Single RT − Dual RT) / Single RT × 100. Higher = greater attentional resource demand. Strobach et al. (2012); Blackwell et al. (2020).

cognitive_reaction_time.label: Cognitive Reaction Time (Dual-Task Mode)
cognitive_reaction_time.notes: D2 T-Mode + concurrent cognitive probe (serial subtraction −3). Dual-task cost ≈20–35% above simple RT for sedentary. Blackwell et al. (2020).

prefrontal_oxygenation.label: Prefrontal Oxygenation (ΔHbO)
prefrontal_oxygenation.notes: ΔHbO during n-back / Stroop task. Positive ΔHbO = prefrontal engagement. Scholkmann et al. (2014); Artinis validation studies.

working_memory.label: Working Memory (NIH List Sorting)
working_memory.notes: List Sorting Working Memory Test. Age/education corrected. Sensitive to hippocampal integrity and prefrontal function. NIH Toolbox CB V3 (2025).

rt_variability.label: Reaction Time Variability (CoV)
rt_variability.notes: CoV = (SD of RT / mean RT) × 100. Intraindividual variability is more sensitive to neurological change than mean RT alone. MacDonald et al. (2006); Stuss et al. (2003).

strength.name: STRENGTHSenses GRFP Force Plates
strength.unit: N, N/kg, N/s, N·s, m/s, cm

strength.name: STRENGTHSenses GRFP Force Plates + Isometric Rig

red_flags.note: Right-side asymmetry detected in lateral step

strength.goal: Increase relative force output and maintain symmetry
strength.progression: Increase load 2.5-5% every 2 weeks when completing all prescribed reps
strength.safety: Monitor symmetry index each session. Stop if index drops below 85%.
strength.retest_trigger: 8 weeks or if symmetry index drops below 88%

movement.goal: Improve movement smoothness and address right-side asymmetry
movement.principle: Motor learning with variability and feedback
movement.progression: Progress from isolated corrections to integrated movement patterns
movement.safety: Avoid end-range loading until symmetry improves. Monitor pain levels.

cardiovascular.goal: Maintain VO2max and improve ventilatory threshold efficiency
cardiovascular.progression: Increase interval duration by 1 min every 2 weeks
cardiovascular.safety: Monitor HR recovery. Flag if recovery drops below 25 bpm at 1 min.
cardiovascular.retest_trigger: 10 weeks or if HR recovery declines

cognitive.goal: Reduce dual-task cost and improve executive function
cognitive.progression: Increase task complexity and speed demands every 2 weeks
cognitive.safety: Monitor fatigue. Stop if accuracy drops below 75% during session.
cognitive.retest_trigger: 6 weeks or if dual-task cost exceeds 25%
